violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety currently licensed with RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Application was completed during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized, ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Tuna salad was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per Pic, tuna was placed in holding unit prior to unit coming to proper temperature. PIC placed tuna salad in freezer to quickly chill. Other foods in unit were observed held at proper temperature. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Staff donned hat during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

Staff observed not changing gloves between using electronic equipment (tablet used for label maker) and handling Ready-to-Eat food (shredded cheese). Staff changed gloves during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
A leak was observed at the self-service beverage machine, with a bucket of water under the counter and water present on shelving. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024

The ceiling tiles in the kitchen area are in poor repair, observed with damage from a previous water leak. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2024
